methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Skyryse, a Series C-stage Space & Aerospace company, carries a current post-money valuation of $1.15B following $500M+ in total raised capital. The valuation analysis triangulates against a comp universe of five public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers, with four same-stage private comparables directly informing the median calculation.

Two methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $1.1B last post-money, implying $2.0B. Peer-median benchmarking across four same-sector, same-stage private companies—Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B)—yields a median of $1.6B and a range of $1.0B to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target company, a typical constraint in private company valuation. The sector's public median trades at 45.3x price-to-sales across six tickers, but this multiple cannot be operationalized here.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a range of $1.4B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.8B. The current $1.15B valuation sits below this range, suggesting potential upside. Key uncertainties include near-term revenue trajectory, capital intensity of the aerospace pathway, and competitive positioning within the peer cohort's wide valuation dispersion.
